content.full_text:
November 17, 2008

Kai-An Yu
Ideal Legal Group
One West Hellman Avenue, Suite 3
Alhambra, California 91803

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. W-08-185

Dear Mr. Yu:

This letter is in response to your request for written advice on behalf of Evie P. Jeang, an attorney with the State Compensation Insurance Fund ("SCIF") regarding the conflict of interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act¹.

Your first question concerns whether SCIF attorneys are required under SCIF's conflict of interest code to report interests outside of their applicable disclosure category on their statement of economic interests (Form 700).

Your second question concerns whether disclosure category 4 of SCIF's conflict of interest code requires disclosure of interests in businesses that contract to receive services from SCIF, or only businesses that contract to provide services to SCIF.

As I informed you in our telephone conversation of November 7, 2008, Regulation 18329.5(a)(2) states that when the Commission is the code reviewing body for the conflict of interest code in question², the individual or his or her authorized representative requesting the advice or assistance regarding interpretation of an agency's conflict of interest code or of the application of that code to a specific individual must first request a determination from the agency

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
² The Commission is the code reviewing body for SCIF.

adopting the conflict of interest code. Thus, we will consider your current request for advice withdrawn and ask you to direct your inquiry to SCIF. After SCIF has provided a reply and assuming the other criteria in Regulation 18329.5 are met, you may, if you wish, resubmit your request to us for advice. We enclose a copy of Regulation 18329.5 for your reference.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Sukhi K. Brar
Commission Counsel
Legal Division

SKB:jgl
Enclosure
